LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS FRITZ QUATTLEBAUM AND BLACK CAT HOSPITALITY GROUP, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO SIX PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF KHALID MUSHASHA IS DENIED AS TO THE ALLEGATION THAT MR. QUATTLEBAUM REMOTELY WATCHED RESTAURANT EMPLOYEES AND GRANTED AS TO ALL OTHER PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN. THE ALLEGATION THAT MR. QUATTLEBAU REMOTELY WATCHED RESTAURANT EMPLOYEES IS PERTINENT TO ONE OF MORE OF MR. MUSHASHA'S CLAIMS. NONE OF THE OTHER PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN IS PERTINENT TO ANY OF MR. MUSHASHA'S CLAIMS. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR#5963, TEL. 707.246.3323. [302]
NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR JUL-16-2018 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON DEC-20-2017 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS FRITZ QUATTLEBAUM AND BLACK CAT HOSPITALITY GROUP, LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF KHALID MUSHASHA IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. MR. MUSHASHA ADEQUATELY ALLEGES ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING EACH OF THE REQUIRED ELEMENTS OF AN IIED AND A DIRECT VICTIM NIED CLAIM, INCLUDING DEFENDANTS' OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AND DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT CAUSED MR. MUSHASHA TO SUFFER SEVERE AND SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. (CACI 1600 AND 1620). COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR#5963, TEL. 707.246.3323. [302]

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-09-2018 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Set For Trial On Dec-03-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Responses To Cross Complainant Black Cat Hospitality Group Llcs Requests For Production And Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by an party or counsel.

The Court vacated the tentative ruling. Defendants Fritz Quattlebaum and Black Cat Hospitality Group, Llc's Motion To Compel Responses To Cross Complainant Black Cat Hospitality Group Llcs Requests For Production And Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions is off calendar per the request of the moving party.

Judge Pro Tem: James H. Fleming, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2017 Set For Trial On Jul-16-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

DEFENDANTS FRITZ QUATTLEBAUM and BLACK CAT HOSPITALITY GROUP, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to six portions of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Khalid Mushasha is DENIED as to the allegation that Mr. Quattlebaum remotely watched restaurant employees and granted as to all other portions of the complaint sought to be stricken. The allegation that Mr. Quattlebau remotely watched restaurant employees is pertinent to one of more of Mr. Mushasha's claims. None of the other portions of the complaint sought to be stricken is pertinent to any of Mr. Mushasha's claims.

Counsel for defendants is to submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR#5963, Tel. 707.246.3323.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

DEFENDANTS FRITZ QUATTLEBAUM and BLACK CAT HOSPITALITY GROUP, LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the ninth and tenth causes of action for intentional and negligent infliction of emotional distress in the complaint filed by plaintiff Khalid Mushasha is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. Mr. Mushasha adequately alleges ultimate facts supporting each of the required elements of an IIED and a direct victim NIED claim, including defendants' outrageous conduct and defendants' conduct caused Mr. Mushasha to suffer severe and serious emotional distress. (CACI 1600 and 1620).

Counsel for defendants is to submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR#5963, Tel. 707.246.3323.
